                  Reliability of the estimates                                                 prehensive and up-to-date discussion of various sources of error, and
                     There are two types of errors possible in an estimate based on a          describes attempts to measure them in the CPS.
                  sample survey—sampling and nonsampling. The standard errors pro-
                  vided primarily indicate the magnitude of the sampling error. They              Sampling error. The standard error is primarily a measure of sampl-
                  also partially measure the effect of some nonsampling errors in              ing variability, that is, of the variation that occurs by chance because a
                  response and enumeration but do not measure any systematic biases in         sample rather than the entire population is surveyed. The sample
                  the data.                                                                    estimate and its estimated standard error enable one to construct con-
